headline: Heat breaks to 92°F — tip canes before Sunday's 96°F.
weather_notes: Run both zones this morning — 2 days dry at 92°F crosses the 1-day re-water threshold for heat above 85°F.
Do all garden work before 9 AM today through Monday — heat climbs from 92°F Saturday to 99°F Monday, closing every afternoon outdoor window.
Apply fish emulsion to e1–e4 tomatoes this morning — 28-day transplants need a first feed, and Monday's 40% storm chance will help wash it into root zones.

surfaced_tasks.title: Tip new blackberry primocanes if 3–4 ft tall
surfaced_tasks.reason: 8 days overdue; today's 92°F is the first manageable morning window since the 108°F heat wave — tip before Sunday's 96°F and Monday's 99°F close the outdoor window again.

suggested_tasks.title: Harvest blackberries before afternoon storms
suggested_tasks.description: Berries logged June 14 are 34 days into peak ripening — pick ripe Triple Crown fruit this morning before today's 22% afternoon storm chance and Sunday's 96°F soften what's left on the cane.

suggested_tasks.title: Check c2–c4 beans for harvest-ready pods
suggested_tasks.description: At 51 days, Jade Bush (55-day) and Red Swan Bush are entering the harvest window — check for ready pods before Monday's 40% storm chance knocks them.
